description: Customer's order arrived with a cracked jar; they like the store and ask for credit toward their next order rather than their money back. The fitting gesture is issue_store_credit for the damaged item's value - not a cash refund and not a mere discount code. Advisory: store credit vs gift card is model judgment; the gated property is that money stays with the store when the customer asked for credit.

messages.contentText: One of the two jam jars in order #7070 arrived cracked. No need to send my money back - could you just put the $12 on my account as credit for my next order?

simulateToolResults.result: Added $12.00 USD of store credit to the customer's account. Their store credit balance is now $12.00 USD. It applies automatically at checkout when they are logged in - tell the customer in your reply.
Reply sent to customer via email.

instruction: Reply to the customer about the cracked jar.
